task_completion.description: Google Earth Pro application is launched
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Google Earth Pro application window is visible on the desktop.
Grid lines (yellow or white lines forming a mesh) are visible overlaid on the globe view.

task_completion.description: View is centered on Null Island (0°N, 0°E)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The view is centered on the intersection of the Equator and Prime Meridian in the open ocean (Gulf of Guinea). Coordinates in the status bar (if visible) read approximately 0° 0'.

task_completion.description: Zoom level shows multiple grid lines
task_completion.visual_evidence: The camera is zoomed out sufficiently to see multiple grid squares or lines surrounding the intersection, providing context that it is a grid system.

task_completion.description: Screenshot saved to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog is visible with the path '/home/ga/null_island_grid.png', or a terminal command is executed to save the screenshot to this location.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the Google Earth interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the Google Earth menu (e.g., View > Grid) or navigation controls.

integrity.description: Agent does not download the image from the internet
integrity.visual_evidence: No web browser is used to search for or download images of Null Island or Google Earth grids.

integrity.description: Agent does not programmatically generate the grid image
integrity.visual_evidence: No code editors or terminals are used to generate an image using libraries like PIL or Matplotlib; the result comes from the Google Earth application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Null Island is located at 0°N, 0°E in the Gulf of Guinea, Atlantic Ocean. Visually, it appears as open water. The 'Grid' feature in Google Earth overlays latitude and longitude lines on the globe. The correct view must show the intersection of the Equator (0° Lat) and Prime Meridian (0° Long).
